issue_title: Followup: JPA Map store wants to use `hibernate.integrator_provider` in Quarkus
issue_body:
### Description

When implementing the JPA Map store in Quarkus, in issue #13200 it was found that the Hibernate Event Listeners didn't register. 

This seems to be due to the property `hibernate.integrator_provider` not being supported in Quarkus, while it worked fine in Undertow. 

While the issue implemented a working solution, it wasn't as isolated as we wished for. This is a follow-up issue to report this upstream and to look for a better solution.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Avoid a global serviceloader, when the event listener should be set up in isolated way for just for the JPA Map storage entitymanagers. 

Having it registered for all entity managers, it might negatively affect other storage implementation which are not JPA Map storage but still use JPA.

### Details

The current service loader class is `EventListenerIntegrator`.
commit_message:
Make the event listeners specific to the persistence unit

Closes #13219

issue_title: Followup: Revisit JTA vs. RESOURCE_LOCAL for JPA map storage for Quarkus and other Setups
issue_body:
### Description

When implementing the JPA Map store in Quarkus, in issue #13200 it was found that the autocommit for the DB connection wasn't disabled.

In the Undertow setup for JPA Map storage, Hibernate disabled the autocommit. In the Quarkus setup with the legacy store this is disabled by the XA transaction manger. 

A workaround in the `QuarkusJpaMapStorageProviderFactory` disables the autocommit. 

A better solution would be to disable it in the Agroal connection pool, as that will reset the autocommit on every returned connection, which seems to be overhead. 

Unfortunately I didn't find an option to change the behavior of any component involved, so there is a workaround in place.


### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Avoid switching the autocommit manually back and forth when retrieving and returning the connection. Ideally it should be handled in the connection pool. 

This is also a solution advocated for here: https://vladmihalcea.com/why-you-should-always-use-hibernate-connection-provider_disables_autocommit-for-resource-local-jpa-transactions/

### Details

_No response_
commit_message:
Changing Quarkus transaction handling for JPA map storage to JTA

This has been recommended as the supported way of transaction handling by the Quarkus team.
Adding handling of exceptions thrown when committing JTA.
Re-adding handling of exceptions when interacting with the entity manager, plus wrapping access to queries to map exceptions during auto-flushing.

Closes #13222

issue_title: Username is removed when updating service account with empty/null email when declarative user profile and registrationEmailAsUsername is enabled
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

What appears to be happening here is that when a UserProfile is auto-constructed for a User on lookup, there is a bug in a check which results in the username being assigned null if email is not set (causing an email attribute to exist but be an empty list).

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/18.0.2/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/userprofile/DefaultAttributes.java#L308

```java
// XXX: email may be non-null but empty list
List<String> email = newAttributes.get(UserModel.EMAIL);

if (email != null && realm.isRegistrationEmailAsUsername()) {
    final List<String> lowerCaseEmailList = email.stream()
            .filter(Objects::nonNull)
            .map(String::toLowerCase)
            .collect(Collectors.toList());
    // XXX: username overwritten with empty list
    newAttributes.put(UserModel.USERNAME, lowerCaseEmailList);
}
```

Then, subsequently when update to the User entity is made, a difference is detected between the current (set) username value and the incoming (now unset because derived from empty email), and the username is annulled

![1-emptyusernameattr](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77031/180072382-88129672-0e43-413f-8994-bd2bb9aafab8.png)

![2-setnullusername](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/77031/180072385-0266bfea-7219-4964-a809-93f64c69c289.png)
.

(screenshots are depicting different users because of separate debug runs, but this sequence can happen to each user)

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

`username` field is not set to null/empty when email is not set for user account, or `registrationEmailAsUsername` is not applied to service account users.

### Actual behavior

`username` field on user is unexpected set from valid value to null.

### How to Reproduce?

use `PUT PUT /admin/realms/{realm}/users/{id}` API to update a service-account user which has a username, but no email, when realm has `"registrationEmailAsUsername": true`

### Anything else?

Discovered on 18.0.0
commit_message:
Ignore user profile attributes for service accounts

Closes #13236

issue_title: Performance Issues with many offline sessions
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We have approx. 6 million offline sessions in the database and enabled lazy loading of offline sessions. 

We ran into performance issues: 

If a user has >= 2 user sessions  and you open the consents tab in the admin UI for this user, the following code tries to load the corresponding offline client sessions: 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/febb447919044943241ad97200821c7da5099cb3/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/session/JpaUserSessionPersisterProvider.java#L383

The where clause: 

sess.userSessionId >= :fromSessionId and sess.userSessionId <= :toSessionId

can cause the query to load lots of client sesssions. 

In our test the user had 2 user sessions with the IDs 156db9e9-d2ad-4168-89ba-21818a475dcc' and 'd4fed476-52c3-44d6-a510-7db0158dc032'

The query tried to load about 3 million client sessions. 

The long-running query leads to infinispan timeout exceptions and can even cause the kubernetes pods to get unhealthy.

The where clause had been changed from an IN-clause to the above BETWEEN-clause in this PR: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/8012/files#diff-9e427660baeacfeb4c94e0dcbede5ae96e7be4355554023fcd204118e75fe40dL269





### Version

15.1.1

### Expected behavior

Offline user sessions are displayed in the user's consent tab and can be revoked.

### Actual behavior

Click on user consents tab causes severe performacance issues and infinispan timeout exceptions and may even cause pods to get unhealthy or the service to crash completely.

### How to Reproduce?

- a lot of offline sessions (> 1 million)
- enable lazy loading of offline sessions
- create 2 offline sessions for 1 user (login with scope offline_access)
- open admin UI, open user, click on consents tab

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Jpa Map Storage: locked user sessions lead to read timeouts
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When running the broker tests, the logout process ends up with exceptions like the one bellow:

```
23:15:40,417 WARN  [org.keycloak.broker.oidc.OIDCIdentityProvider] Failed backchannel broker logout to: https://localhost:8543/auth/realms/provider/protocol/openid-connect/logout?state=2af21a4b-5222-452e-9339-cd76999045a8&id_token_hint=eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCIgOiAiSldUIiwia2lkIiA6ICJhWWxYbVFhbERjVWM1d2JFNVBON0RKSURGRTQzXzNvZEpJQ2cyNFlFaWxRIn0..XUR4DCJ_wRWOFz_SU3kfFNyUEx7h8aqr_qn7SJ2oshnSTzGqPJFqLBJMrV-nwT2OefcZPly8NphmkvC_xzoJyqSbaY8gY-UbUaGZyd4OZPGSySL8V-aEQHQoKhSC5L6xn0_e-FgFDMgmrmtjlc_tVen4n4-pYxnf9V8LuP973IrZ5zTrRd4RMV9FxLH2WaOu3CrYv581LlSxEdz6bAlkKKz9L500QkokHdUT7qdoWTCykkVujtYlz614-VmUmeEvCEvzB8r_pcUzyQPADEP00r7SnpldzzrEQHVc5Oy0cqzb_RRcAYGK_j61iRB2oV1w0EuaiXUdH2kcagy8P3nDfA
java.net.SocketTimeoutException: Read timed out
	at java.base/java.net.SocketInputStream.socketRead0(Native Method)
	at java.base/java.net.SocketInputStream.socketRead(SocketInputStream.java:115)
	at java.base/java.net.SocketInputStream.read(SocketInputStream.java:168)
	at java.base/java.net.SocketInputStream.read(SocketInputStream.java:140)
```

And this happens because the requests exchanged between the `consumer` and `producer` logout endpoints attempt to lock the same user session:

1. `AbstractBaseBrokerTest.logoutRealm` ends up calling the `LogoutEndpoint.logoutConfirmAction` on the `consumer` endpoint, which proceeds with the browser logout that locks the user session and that at some point sends a logout request to the `provider` endpoint. Relevant call stack up to this point:
```
	at org.keycloak.broker.oidc.OIDCIdentityProvider.backchannelLogout(OIDCIdentityProvider.java:139)
	at org.keycloak.broker.oidc.OIDCIdentityProvider.keycloakInitiatedBrowserLogout(OIDCIdentityProvider.java:155)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.browserLogout(AuthenticationManager.java:649)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.initiateBrowserLogout(LogoutEndpoint.java:750)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.doBrowserLogout(LogoutEndpoint.java:447)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.logoutConfirmAction(LogoutEndpoint.java:376)
```

2. The `provider` logout endpoint proceeds with the logout until it sends a `k_logout` request back to the `consumer`. Relevant stack:
```
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.ResourceAdminManager.sendLogoutRequest(ResourceAdminManager.java:301)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.ResourceAdminManager.logoutClientSessions(ResourceAdminManager.java:167)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.ResourceAdminManager.logoutClientSession(ResourceAdminManager.java:123)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.OIDCLoginProtocol.backchannelLogout(OIDCLoginProtocol.java:342)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.backchannelLogoutClientSession(AuthenticationManager.java:509)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.backchannelLogoutAll(AuthenticationManager.java:393)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.backchannelLogout(AuthenticationManager.java:296)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.AuthenticationManager.backchannelLogout(AuthenticationManager.java:253)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.doBrowserLogout(LogoutEndpoint.java:458)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.LogoutEndpoint.logout(LogoutEndpoint.java:307)
```
3. This request is now handled by the `KeycloakOIDCIdentityProvider.backchannelLogout` endpoint, and at this method, in line 105, attempts to lock the user session
 
```
                    UserSessionModel userSession = lockUserSessionsForModification(session, () -> session.sessions().getUserSessionByBrokerSessionId(realm, brokerSessionId));
```

The request is now waiting for the user session that was locked in step 1, and the deadlock is only undone when the `Read timed out` exception is thrown, wihch allows the first request to proceed and release the lock.

This happens with both PostgreSQL and CockroachDB.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

Broker logout logic shouldn't get stuck on a deadlock where multiple requests are trying to lock the same user sessions

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

1- Setup CockroachDB according to the instructions in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/9596
2- Using the IDE, run any broker test (for example, `ExternalKeycloakRoleToRoleMapperTest`) with the following VM options:

```
-Dpageload.timeout=90000
-Dkeycloak.profile.feature.map_storage=enabled
-Dkeycloak.authSession.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.authorization.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.client.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.clientScope.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.eventsStore.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.group.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.loginFailure.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.realm.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.role.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.actionToken.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.singleUseObject.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.user.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.userSession.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.userSession.map.storage.provider=jpa
-Dkeycloak.publicKeyStorage.provider=map
-Dkeycloak.realmCache.enabled=false
-Dkeycloak.userCache.enabled=false
-Dkeycloak.authorizationCache.enabled=false
-Dkeycloak.publicKeyCache.enabled=false
-Dkeycloak.userFederatedStorage.provider=
-Dkeycloak.userSessionPersister.provider=
-Dkeycloak.dblock.provider=none
-Dkeycloak.map.storage.connectionsJpa.url=jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/keycloak
-Dkeycloak.map.storage.connectionsJpa.user=keycloak
-Dkeycloak.map.storage.connectionsJpa.password=pass
```

For CRDB adjust the connection port to 26257

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Unable to configure URL scheme for admin console
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I'm trying to setup Keycloak 18.0.2 in a reverse proxy environment where the HTTPS connection is terminated at the proxy. The admin console is also exposed via the proxy. However, after logging in to the admin console, some requests are blocked with a "Mixed content" error from the browser.

The reason for this appears to be that the scheme of the `authUrl` JavaScript variable is set to `http`:

```
var authServerUrl = 'https://MY-URL/auth';
var authUrl = 'http://MY-URL/auth'; // <-- INCORRECT URL SCHEME
...
```

I could not find a way to configure this. Looking through the code, this line seems suspect: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/8470a30446cf1a8280353fb96f8e2152d5d186ac/quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/hostname/DefaultHostnameProvider.java#L67

A recent commit has added the possibility to configure the admin hostname (commit b34f4615, added in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/12192). Would it be possible to add a similar configuration option for the admin scheme?


### Version

18.0.2
commit_message:
Allow setting a URL to configure frontend and admin URLs

Closes #13524

Co-authored-by: Stian Thorgersen <stianst@gmail.com>

issue_title: locale attribute not set after registration
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When a user creates a new account using the direct registration link of our application the user's locale is not saved as an attribute. If the user explicitly switches the current language by the language dropdown, the attribute is set, even if they just switch to english and back to german.

See also [this SO question](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/66208538/keycloak-locale-not-set-after-registration) describing the very same behaviour.

The registration link in our app is like this

https://<server>/auth/realms/<realm>/protocol/openid-connect/registrations?client_id=<id>&response_type=code&ui_locales=de&kc_locale=de&redirect_uri=<callback_url>

as mentioned [here ](https://stackoverflow.com/a/53264417/2217962) and in the referred keycloak mailing list archive.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

Every user should have a "locale" attribute after registration, containing the language used during registration.

### Actual behavior

"locale" attribute is not set at all.

### How to Reproduce?

* Start keycloak
* Use registration link from above
* Register a new user
* Go to user detail page - locale dropdown is empty
* Read user attrbutes - locale is not set

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Propagate locale when using app initiated registration URL

Fixes #13505

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

issue_title: Exceptions are not visible in testsuite output
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I'm running the test suite under maven with `-Pauth-server-quarkus`. 
Exceptions in the server are not visible in the log output.

The server logs are also missing from the testsuite output.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

When code run by the server throws an exception, lets say `NullPointerException`, I'd like full backtrace to be displayed in the test suite output.

### Actual behavior

The exception from failing test case is shown, but the original exception in the server that caused the test case to fail is not shown.

### How to Reproduce?

Modify server to throw unhandled exception.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Misleading/unhelpful error for invalid SAML responses
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When an invalid SAML response is received during login, the error displayed is the unhelpful:

> We are sorry...
> invalidFederatedIdentityActionMessage

There are 2 issues with this:

1. The message key ("invalidFederatedIdentityActionMessage") is displayed instead of the message from properties file ("Invalid or missing action")
2. The message (key or text) is an inaccurate description of the problem

### Version

8.0.2, 15.1.1

### Expected behavior

Error message displayed when receiving invalid SAML response:
> We are sorry...
> Invalid response from identity provider.

Error message displayed when receiving SAML response with invalid signature:
> We are sorry...
> Invalid signature in response from identity provider.


### Actual behavior

Error message displayed when receiving invalid SAML response or response with invalid signature:
> We are sorry...
> invalidFederatedIdentityActionMessage


### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure Keycloak with a SAML Identity Provider with an invalid or an expired Validating X509 Certificate and Validate Signature turned ON.
2. Attempt to log in using that Identity Provider.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Improve error messages for invalid SAML responses

Closes #13534

issue_title: Preview Feature "Update Email" clears first and last name upon email confirmation
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When the new UPDATE_EMAIL feature is enabled, going through the process of changing your email address clears the first and last name fields of your user profile.

### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

Changing the email address should not affect any other profile fields.

### Actual behavior

After clicking the confirmation link in the email, the email address gets successfully updated (as expected), but at the same time the first and last name are cleared.

### How to Reproduce?

* Enable the UPDATE_EMAIL feature (e.g. KC_FEATURES=update-email)
* Create a realm
  * which has the "Update Email" required action enabled
  * which has email verification enabled
* Create a user with an email address, first and last name
* Log in as that user and go to the account console. Observe the email address, first and last name in the profile information.
* Click "Update email" and enter a new (different) email adress
* Click the confirmation link sent to that email adress
* Go to the account console again. The email address has been updated, but the first and last name are gone.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not empty attributes if they are not provided when user profile is enabled

Closes #11096

issue_title: New Update Email flow not working when "User Profile Enabled" enabled from realm.
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When "User Profile Enabled" is disabled and update email is enabled , first name and last name became empty on clicking the email validation link. It clears the user profile attributes which were not sent.

But once we enable the "User Profile Enabled" from Realm General setting, we are getting error while confirming new email id on update.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15565805/183021290-2d365794-4af2-4002-ae8b-102654e77923.png)




### Version

V19.0.1

### Expected behavior

Only email id should be updated. It should work fine with "User Profile Enabled" enabled or disabled.

### Actual behavior

With "User Profile Enabled" disabled, first name and last name became empty.

With "User Profile Enabled" enabled, getting error "Please specify this field"

### How to Reproduce?

From the realm disable "User Profile Enabled" and enable "verify email". Go to user account profile and update email address. On clicking email confirmation link, first name and last name is removed from profile.

From the realm enable "User Profile Enabled" and enable "verify email".  Go to user account profile and update email address, getting error "Please specify this field"

### Anything else?

Only email id should be updated and no other use profile fields should be checked and updated

issue_title: Reuse of token in TOTP is possible
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

According to the document, the verifier should not accept the second attempt of the OTP after successful validation but KeyCloak is allowing using of the same OTP token multiple times within the timeframe.  

https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6238.html#section-5

Note that a prover may send the same OTP inside a given time-step window
multiple times to a verifier. The verifier MUST NOT accept the second
attempt of the OTP after the successful validation has been issued for the
first OTP, which ensures one-time only use of an OTP.

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

_No response_

_No response_

_No response_

_No response_

issue_title: Offline User Session dead lock caused by PersisterLastSessionRefreshStore
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

There is a deadlock under some circumstances with latest Keycloak.

In case there are 2 cluster nodes and both updating DB at the same time from persister:

Node1 calling something like (real Ids replaced with placeholders):
```
update OFFLINE_USER_SESSION set LAST_SESSION_REFRESH=1656332405 where REALM_ID='X' and 
OFFLINE_FLAG='1' and (USER_SESSION_ID in ('a' , 'b' , 'c' , 'd'))
```
Node2 calling something like (real Ids replaced with placeholders):
```
update OFFLINE_USER_SESSION set LAST_SESSION_REFRESH=1656332405 where REALM_ID='Y' and 
OFFLINE_FLAG='1' and (USER_SESSION_ID in ('aa' , 'bb' , 'cc' , 'dd'))
```
From the lock monitor, both are named JPQL "updateUserSessionLastSessionRefresh" called from different RH-SSO node.

JPQL "updateUserSessionLastSessionRefresh" definition:
```
update PersistentUserSessionEntity sess set lastSessionRefresh = :lastSessionRefresh where sess.realmId = :realmId AND 
sess.offline = :offline AND sess.userSessionId IN (:userSessionIds)
```
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/19.0.1/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/session/PersistentUserSessionEntity.java#L38-L39

When token refresh or token introspection is requested to an offline session, RH-SSO need to update LAST_SESSION_REFRESH culumn of the OFFLINE_USER_SESSION table record,
but instead of issuing the update query immediately, RH-SSO enqueue the update request in AbstractLastSessionRefreshStore, and update them periodically.
JPQL "updateUserSessionLastSessionRefresh" is the query for this bulk update.

Since token refresh or token introspection requests from clients are distributed to RH-SSO nodes,
it may happen that multiple LAST_SESSION_REFRESH update queries are issued from different RH-SSO nodes at the same time.
If it happens to multiple sessions, it ends up in the dead lock because RH-SSO node wraps the multiple update request into one transaction.

### Dead lock flow example

If clients requests offline sessions X and Y to different RH-SSO nodes(A and B), RH-SSO Node A and B issues update queries in one transaction. This will cause dead lock. 

```
RH-SSO Node A ----> Begin Tx ----> Update Realm001 session X ---------------> Update Realm002 session Y  -----------> Dead 
Lock!(Waiting for Realm002 session Y)
RH-SSO Node B --------> Begin Tx ------------> Update Realm002 session Y -------------> Update Realm001 session X  -> Dead 
Lock!(Waiting for Realm001 session X)
```

### Solution proposal

Sorting the session update order will resolve the dead lock.

I think sorting the items by key here will work.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/19.0.1/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/sessions/infinispan/changes/sessions/PersisterLastSessionRefreshStore.java#L64
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/19.0.1/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/sessions/infinispan/changes/sessions/PersisterLastSessionRefreshStore.java#L72

```
RH-SSO Node A ----> Begin Tx ----> Update Realm001 session X ---------------> Update Realm002 session Y ---> Commit ---> 
Finish!
RH-SSO Node B --------> Begin Tx ------------> Update Realm001 session X ------> (Wait for Realm001 session X lock) -----> 
Update Realm002 session Y --> Commit --> Finish!
``` 

Could you check my analysis? and if it valid, could you provide a fix and include it to the next RH-SSO 7.6 patch update?


### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

This is related to ticket 293.

Originally reported by @ynojima
commit_message:
Use separate transactions for each bulk update of offline sessions in PersisterLastSessionRefreshStore to avoid deadlocks
closes #13684

issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When their apps are refreshing the access token using the offline tokens, they are noticing 'CONSTRAINT_OFFL_CL_SES_PK3' violation on the OFFLINE_CLIENT_SESSION table.

Logs:

[org.hibernate.engine.jdbc.spi.SqlExceptionHelper] (default task-173) Violation of PRIMARY KEY constraint 'CONSTRAINT_OFFL_CL_SES_PK3'. Cannot insert duplicate key in object 'dbo.OFFLINE_CLIENT_SESSION'. The duplicate key value is (<some-uuid>, <another-uuid>, local, local, 1).

Customer upgraded RHSSO from 7.3.8 to 7.5.0 on June 2nd and then patched it to 7.5.2.

Customer is looking to find out why this error happens?

Customer investigated and believes this is
```
    happening when the client session is expired in infinispan cache but is not yet removed in the
offline_client_session table. That is when I believe it will try to make an entry into offline_client_session
table with the existing user_sessoion_id and client_id
```


### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

This comes from the support ticket 281

issue_title: CryptoIntegration fails to init on WebSphere Liberty
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

In 19.0.0 and later versions the **PemUtils** and **CertUtils** classes fail to initialize on WebSphere Liberty with the following exception:

> Caused by: java.lang.ExceptionInInitializerError
> 	at java.lang.J9VMInternals.ensureError(J9VMInternals.java:145)
> 	at java.lang.J9VMInternals.recordInitializationFailure(J9VMInternals.java:134)
> 	at org.keycloak.adapters.KeycloakDeploymentBuilder.internalBuild(KeycloakDeploymentBuilder.java:69)
> 	at org.keycloak.adapters.KeycloakDeploymentBuilder.build(KeycloakDeploymentBuilder.java:205)
> 	at ***.iseries.api.tokenutils.TokenRetriever.<init>(TokenRetriever.java:51)
> 	at ***.iseries.api.tokenutils.TokenRetriever.<init>(TokenRetriever.java:55)
> 	at ***.iseries.tokenserver.Worker.post(Worker.java:74)
> 	at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
> 	at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:90)
> 	at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:55)
> 	at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:508)
> 	at com.ibm.ejs.container.interceptors.InterceptorProxy.invokeInterceptor(InterceptorProxy.java:200)
> 	at com.ibm.ejs.container.interceptors.InvocationContextImpl.proceed(InvocationContextImpl.java:585)
> 	at com.ibm.ws.cdi.ejb.impl.InterceptorChain.proceed(InterceptorChain.java:119)
> 	at com.ibm.ws.cdi.ejb.impl.EJBCDIInterceptorWrapper.invokeInterceptors(EJBCDIInterceptorWrapper.java:131)
> 	at com.ibm.ws.cdi.ejb.impl.EJBCDIInterceptorWrapper.postConstruct(EJBCDIInterceptorWrapper.java:67)
> 	at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
> 	at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:90)
> 	at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:55)
> 	at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:508)
> 	at com.ibm.ejs.container.interceptors.InterceptorProxy.invokeInterceptor(InterceptorProxy.java:189)
> 	at com.ibm.ejs.container.interceptors.InvocationContextImpl.proceed(InvocationContextImpl.java:577)
> 	at org.jboss.weld.ejb.AbstractEJBRequestScopeActivationInterceptor.aroundInvoke(AbstractEJBRequestScopeActivationInterceptor.java:73)
> 	at com.ibm.ws.cdi.ejb.interceptor.WeldSessionBeanInterceptorWrapper.aroundInvoke(WeldSessionBeanInterceptorWrapper.java:58)
> 	at com.ibm.ws.cdi.ejb.interceptor.WeldSessionBeanInterceptorWrapper.postConstruct(WeldSessionBeanInterceptorWrapper.java:67)
> 	at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
> 	at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:90)
> 	at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:55)
> 	at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:508)
> 	at com.ibm.ejs.container.interceptors.InterceptorProxy.invokeInterceptor(InterceptorProxy.java:189)
> 	at com.ibm.ejs.container.interceptors.InvocationContextImpl.proceed(InvocationContextImpl.java:577)
> 	at com.ibm.ejs.container.interceptors.InvocationContextImpl.doLifeCycle(InvocationContextImpl.java:326)
> 	at com.ibm.ejs.container.SingletonBeanO.callTransactionalLifecycleInterceptors(SingletonBeanO.java:221)
> 	... 24 more
> Caused by: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Not able to load any cryptoProvider with the classLoader: sun.misc.Launcher$AppClassLoader@bf56248d
> 	at org.keycloak.common.crypto.CryptoIntegration.detectProvider(CryptoIntegration.java:52)
> 	at org.keycloak.common.crypto.CryptoIntegration.init(CryptoIntegration.java:27)
> 	at org.keycloak.common.util.PemUtils.<clinit>(PemUtils.java:41)
> 	... 55 more


I believe this is because they use system classloader which is not aware of application provided classes because WebSphere uses dedicated application class loader to load it's classes.

The error occurs in **detectProvider** method of **CryptoIntegration** class (stream is empty cause of service loader not loading default implementation):

```
    private static CryptoProvider detectProvider(ClassLoader classLoader) {
        List<CryptoProvider> foundProviders = StreamSupport.stream(ServiceLoader.load(CryptoProvider.class, classLoader).spliterator(), false)
                .collect(Collectors.toList());

        if (foundProviders.isEmpty()) {
            throw new IllegalStateException("Not able to load any cryptoProvider with the classLoader: " + classLoader);
        } else if (foundProviders.size() > 1) {
            throw new IllegalStateException("Multiple crypto providers loaded with the classLoader: " + classLoader +
                    ". Make sure only one cryptoProvider available on the classpath. Available providers: " +foundProviders);
        } else {
            logger.infof("Detected crypto provider: %s", foundProviders.get(0).getClass().getName());
            return foundProviders.get(0);
        }
    }
```

Shouldn't **PemUtils** and **CertificateUtils** classes be initialized exactly the same as **KeycloakDeploymentBuilder**?

`CryptoIntegration.init(KeycloakDeploymentBuilder.class.getClassLoader());`

instead of

`    static {
        CryptoIntegration.init(ClassLoader.getSystemClassLoader());
    }`

### Version

19.0.0

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Initialize CryptoIntegration using the same classloader that was used to load CertificateUtils and PemUtils classes

Closes #13803

issue_title: Can't set storage-deployment-state-version-seed for map storage via environment variable
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When using the map storage for Quarkus, I can successfully set the CLI option `--storage-deployment-state-version-seed`, then no warning is issued in StoragePropertyMappers. 

Once I try to set it via an environment variable `KC_STORAGE_DEPLOYMENT_STATE_VERSION_SEED` this doesn't end in the parameterValue passed to `getResourcesVersionSeed()`: 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/8470a30446cf1a8280353fb96f8e2152d5d186ac/quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/configuration/mappers/StoragePropertyMappers.java#L322-L329

I seems to me that I misunderstood how the configuration mechanism works here. 

I tried to double-check the https://www.keycloak.org/server/all-config page, but it seems to be on 19.0.0 and not 19.0.1 as this option was backported.

### Version

19.0.1-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

The option should be configurable with an environment variable, as this is the way the Keycloak operator passes on information to Keycloak.


### Actual behavior

The warning is logged on the CLI

> Version seed for deployment state set with a random number. Caution: This can lead to unstable operations when serving resources from the cluster without a sticky loadbalancer or when restarting nodes. Set the '--storage-deployment-state-version-seed' option with a secret seed to ensure stable operations.

Surprisingly enough when debugging, the environment option still ends in the provider. 


### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Remove warning from StoragePropertyMappers about the deployment state version seed

It duplicates the logic in the provider and is incomplete. A follow-up issue will investigate how a provider can defer a configuration option.

Closes #13807

issue_title: Switching Locale after Completing an admin triggered required action yields an error
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

One way to produce this is to

- install Keycloak
- login to admin console and create new realm "test" . Perform operations below with the new realm
- configure mail server
- configure more than one locale for the current theme in the "test" realm
- create user with email address
- as an admin go to users -> the new user -> credentials
- Trigger a credential reset with the reset action VERIFY_EMAIL
- Wait for the email to arrive
- Open the link from the email
- click on the button "Click here to proceed" to complete the required action
- now switch to another locale
- there you are with an error page for "Cookie not found. Please make sure cookies are enabled in your browser"

In the log, there is this:
```
2022-08-22 18:04:20,178 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-61) type=CUSTOM_REQUIRED_ACTION_ERROR, realmId=41bda29c-2586-45f1-bc08-f072929fddf7, clientId=null, userId=null, ipAddress=127.0.0.1, error=cookie_not_found
```



### Version

19.0.1

### Expected behavior

2 possibilities how to fix it:

1) Locale is switched. Ideally it keeps to be switched for the current user, who triggered the action (if possible). Or it is kept as a KC_LOCALE in the browser

2) The alternative solution is, that on the "Info" page (the page displayed after user clicked on the `Click here to proceed`), there won't be a choice to switch the locale.

The solution (1) is better, but not sure it is possible. As I think that after click `Click here to proceed`, there is not anymore any authenticationSession available and hence this will require changing locale without authenticationSession available, which might be very tricky (or impossible) to do.

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix updating locale on info/error page after authenticationSession was already removed
Closes #13922
